'''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006.== See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:return the see also]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006.== See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.

'''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. revision.text:
'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 2]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans [[Category:pp error]]approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|12=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線|12=draft}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|12=draft}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権|12=draft}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬|12=draft}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]
{{citation needed|Nagahara Kofun Cluster}}

<!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->
<!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->
<!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|12=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線|12=draft}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|12=draft}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|12=draft}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|12=draft}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権|12=draft}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬|12=draft}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]
{{citation needed|Nagahara Kofun Cluster}} <!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->
<!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->
<!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->
<!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->
<!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->
<!--Nagahara Kofun Cluster-->
長原古墳群]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = 長原古墳群 | native_name = | native_language = | image =Nagahara Kofun Group, sekihi.jpg | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | caption =長原古墳群 石碑（[[出戸駅]]前） | type =古墳群 | coordinates ={{coord|34|36|30.8|N|135|33|55.6|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | location =[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[平野区]]長吉長原 | area = | built = | architect = | architecture = | governing_body = | owner = | designation1 = | designation1_offname = | designation1_date = | designation1_number = }} {{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture| label = 長原古墳群| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Center|位置図}}}} '''長原古墳群'''（ながはらこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[平野区]]長吉長原とその周辺の地下に埋没して存在する[[古墳群]]。[[古墳時代]]前期末から後期初めにかけて築かれた[[群集墳]]である。[[大阪市営地下鉄]]工事により存在が明らかになり、200基以上の古墳、古墳の痕跡が検出され、多数の[[埴輪]]が出土していることで有名となっている。 == 長原古墳群の発見と調査の経緯== 1973年（昭和48年）、[[大阪市営地下鉄]][[大阪市営地下鉄谷町線|谷町線]]延長計画に伴う長原遺跡の発掘調査で、地上には何ら古墳の痕跡がないのに[[円筒埴輪]]などが次々と出土した。調査を続けると墳丘の盛土の多くは失われているものの、墳裾に周溝を持つ小規模な古墳の痕跡が多数残っていることが判明した長山（1988）pp.400 - 401。 次いで、直径50メートル以上、残存部で2段に築かれた墳丘が姿を現し、その地区の小字名が塚ノ本であることから、塚ノ本古墳（円墳）と名付けられた。地籍図を調べると他にも、一ヶ塚、落塚、狐塚、高塚など周囲にかつて古墳が存在したことをうかがわせる地名が存在し、その範囲は南北2キロメートル、東西1.5キロメートルにも及んでいた長山（1988）pp.401 - 402。 これらの範囲では市営地下鉄工事の後も[[近畿自動車道]]の建設、区画整理事業、下水道工事、住宅建設、建築の建て替え工事などに伴い、続々と古墳の[[遺構]]が調査され、2006年までに調査、検出された数は213基にも及んでいる寺井（2006）p.273。 == 古墳群の形成時期== これらの古墳から出土した埴輪や[[須恵器]]の[[編年]]観から、4世紀末頃から6世紀前半にかけての期間にこの古墳群が形成されたと推定されており、これを基に4期にわたる変遷が説かれている長山（1988）p.405高島（1989）p.424。1期（4世紀末頃）には塚ノ本古墳や一ヶ塚古墳（[[帆立貝形古墳]]）など、直径数十メートルの比較的大型の古墳が築かれ、その周囲に2期から3期にかけて小規模な古墳（方墳）が多数築かれている。4期（6世紀前半頃）には[[前方後円墳]]の七ノ坪古墳や、南口古墳が築かれたのを最後にこの古墳群の造営は終了したのではないかとされる長山（1988）pp.404 - 415寺井（2006）pp.274 - 275。5世紀代とみられる2期と3期のものが多数を占めており、[[奈良県]][[新沢千塚古墳群]]などと同じく初期[[群集墳]]の1つに位置づけることが出来る長山（1988）pp.448 - 449。また長原古墳群が、南東わずか数キロに位置する日本最大級の古墳群、[[古市古墳群]]の造営時期とほぼ重なることから、両古墳群の関連も指摘されているところである。この事象と関連して、1期に比較的大型の盟主的古墳が築かれているのに2期から3期にはもっぱら小型の方墳ばかり築かれるのは、この地域の支配構造が1期は在地首長を通じて[[大王]]の間接的な支配を受けるものであったのが、2-3期には大王権による直接支配へと変化したことによるのではないかとする説もある大庭（2006）p.1。また、さらに進んで、長原古墳群の小形方墳の被葬者を[[ヤマト王権]]に組み込まれた原初的な「官僚」、初現的官僚組織に属した人々とする考えもある三木（2011）p.239。　 == 内部構造と遺物== [[ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 船形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|169号墳（高廻り1号墳）出土
船形埴輪（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|[[大阪歴史博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 船形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|170号墳（高廻り2号墳）出土
船形埴輪（国の重要文化財）}}{{small|大阪歴史博物館展示。}}]] 長原古墳群では後世の削平が著しいため主体部（遺体が収められている内部構造）が残っているケースは少なく、その構造が明らかになっているのは18 - 19基である。それらは[[木棺直葬]]　[[円筒棺|埴輪棺]]（大型の円筒埴輪を棺に転用したものやあるいは遺体を収めるために作られた大型の埴輪）が主で、[[粘土槨]]も1例知られる。[[竪穴式石室]]や[[石棺]]の存在は現在のところ知られていないが、最後の時期に属する七ノ坪古墳（130号墳）からは[[横穴式石室]]の基底部が検出されている。 副葬品検出例は同じ理由で少ないが、鉄剣、鉄斧、鉄鎌などの鉄製品、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]、[[管玉]]などが出ており、上記、七ノ坪古墳の石室底部から馬具類一式がガラス玉、鉄製武器などともに出土している寺井（2006）p.275。 また、これらの古墳の墳丘の残存部や周溝から埴輪が多数出土しており、円筒埴輪、壺形埴輪の他に、人物、馬、[[盾]]、[[甲冑]]、蓋（きぬがさ）などの[[形象埴輪]]、囲形、舟形、家形の埴輪が出土している。 なかでも高廻り1号墳、2号墳（長原古墳群の支群の古墳群）から出土した船形、短甲形、冑形などの埴輪30点は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている京嶋（2007）p.29「新指定の文化財」『月刊文化財』号、第一法規、1992、pp.17 - 20, 42。 1号墳の船形埴輪は[[宮崎県]] [[西都原古墳群]]などで出土しているものと同じく、船首、船尾が反り上がったゴンドラ形をしていた。2号墳からは別のタイプの船の埴輪が出土しており、船首、船尾に波除板を付けるもので、隣接する[[八尾市]][[久宝寺遺跡]]より出土した木造船（準構造船）と同じ構造であった京嶋（2007）pp.29 - 30。 両古墳が検出された場所は現在、児童公園とされ、なみはや公園と呼ばれている。これは大阪市が市制100周年事業の1つとして2号墳の船形埴輪をモデルに復原船が製作され、公募によってこの船が「なみはや」と命名され、1989年（平成元年）に韓国釜山まで実験航海を行なったことに由来する（大阪[[天保山]]から出航し、約700キロの航海をもっぱら手漕ぎで、35日かかったという）。両古墳出土の埴輪の実物は[[大阪歴史博物館]]に展示されており、復原船「なみはや」の方は、[[なにわの海の時空館]]に展示されている。また、同公園には長原高廻り古墳群の顕彰碑が同市によって建てられている京嶋（2007）p.30。古墳時代、海と繋がる[[河内湖]]は奥深く内陸に入り込んでおり、長原の地も[[瀬戸内海]]や外洋と直接、繋がっていたようである。 File:Namihaya in Osaka Maritime Museum-2.jpg|古代船「なみはや」
{{small|[[なにわの海の時空館]]展示。}} ==調査で明らかにされた主要な古墳== ;塚ノ本古墳　　 :部分的な発掘調査であるが、直径55メートルの円墳で、周溝の幅は15メートルの規模であることが判明している。埋葬施設や葺石は確認されていないが、墳丘には埴輪が並んでおり、円筒・朝顔形・家形埴輪が出土している。家形埴輪は立派な円柱を持ち、2階建てで高さ80センチになる。長原古墳群では最も古い4世紀末の築造と推定される。古墳周溝のすぐ外では[[円筒棺]]や[[土壙墓]]が確認されている。これらの墓の中には勾玉、鉄釧などの装身具を有するものもあった。被葬者は塚ノ本古墳の被葬者に従属する立場にあった人々と思われる。 ;一ケ塚古墳 :部分的な調査しか行われていないが、方形の[[造出し]]を有する直径47メートルの円墳（または帆立貝形古墳）であり、造出しを含めると全長53メートルの規模である。周溝の幅は14メートルある。埋葬施設は確認されていないが、多種多様な埴輪が周溝から出土している。家形埴輪は7点以上出土しており、その1つは高さ80センチに復元される2階建ての入母屋造りである。この他、盾、[[靭]]、[[鞆]]、[[短甲]]、草摺、蓋などの形象埴輪も多く出土している。被葬者を来世に送る葬送儀礼を墳丘上に再現したものとされる。5世紀初頭頃の古墳と推定されている寺井（2007）pp.88-89。 ;七ノ坪古墳　 :全長約33メートルの帆立貝形の前方後円墳と推定されている。当古墳群のほとんどは墳丘を削られ主体部が失われている場合が多いが、横穴式石室の床部分が辛うじて残り、そこから馬具、鉄製武器、玉類、須恵器が出土した。石室は近畿地方に、横穴式石室の構造が伝えられた初期の形式に属する片袖式の構造である。 ;南口古墳　 :七ノ坪古墳と同じく帆立貝形の前方後円墳で全長は34．5メートルを測る。周溝から埴輪や須恵器、韓式系土器、土師器、刀形木製品、それに馬の骨が出土している。埴輪は円筒埴輪、人物埴輪、武人埴輪、家形埴輪、馬形埴輪、器財埴輪（甲冑、盾　蓋）が見られる。馬骨は両脚の四肢の部分がそのまま据えられた状態で検出され、墳丘の前方部裾部分からは歯の部分も見つかっている。これらのことから周溝内で馬の首を切り、その首を墳丘上においたことが想像され、馬供儀と呼ばれる葬送儀礼が行われたことを示唆している。このような例は長野県など古代の[[牧 (古代)|牧]]（馬の飼育場）があったとされる場所に近い地域の中小規模古墳に多くみられるものである加藤（2007）pp.94-95。 :[[平安時代]]、[[河内国]]にあった皇室の[[御牧]]の1つ、会賀牧（えがまき）が長原付近にあったという説がある京嶋（2008）pp.102-103。長原古墳群の周辺に広がる長原遺跡（旧石器時代から中世にまたがる[[複合遺跡]]で長原古墳群もこの遺跡に含まれる）からは[[治暦]]2年（1066年）の年号のある、土地の帰属をめぐる係争に関する[[木簡]]の文書が出土しており、このなかには「長原里」の地名などとともに末尾に「會賀御□」の記載がある。このことから現在の長原付近が、平安時代頃には、すでに長原里と呼ばれていることが分かり、さらに長原里が広大な[[皇室領]]であった会賀牧の範囲に含まれていたことが推定される寺井（2007）pp.137-142。長原から南に2キロに恵我之荘（えがのしょう-[[羽曳野市]]恵我之荘）の地名が現在も見られる。長原古墳群付近では古墳時代の村の跡からも馬の存在を示す歯や骨が出土し、また古墳群からも馬形埴輪が多く出土しているので長原付近には古墳時代にも馬の飼育や輸送・交通をつかさどる馬飼いと呼ばれる人々が住んでいたと思われる。 [[File:Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|長原高廻り古墳群
（なみはや公園）}}]] ;長原高廻り1・2号墳 :（上記内部構造と遺物の欄および外部リンク参照） ;87号墳 :墳丘の北辺の長さ12.0メートルの方墳で、墳丘の高さは1.0メートルまで残存する。幅3.0メートル、長さ2.0メートル造り出しがあり、造り出しには円筒埴輪列が残っていた。西辺には須恵器大甕を転用した主体部が検出されているが棺内（大甕）からは副葬品や人骨は検出されていない。埴輪には円筒埴輪以外に、蓋、馬、人物、鶏、草摺などの形象埴輪が出土した。人物は巫女で丸顔をし、衣を右肩から垂らし、左腕を前方にのばし、何かを捧げもっているようである。馬形埴輪は顔と脚部分が欠損するが、鞍や障泥、輪鐙、面繋、手綱の表現がある飾り馬である。鶏形埴輪は雄鶏を実物大で作っており、鶏冠や目は粘土を貼り付け、羽や足は線刻で表現されている寺井（2006）pp.19-24。 ;117号墳 :1辺が10-11メートルの方墳で、周溝幅が最大で5メートルある。[[陶邑窯]]出土の須恵器の編年TK208型式に該当する須恵器坏身、坏蓋、耳両付短頸壺、壺が出土した。耳両付短頸壺は朝鮮半島南部（[[全羅道]]）に系譜が求められる形態である。長原2期に位置づけられる寺井（2006）p.65。 ;213号墳 :大阪市文化財協会が2002年度に発掘した1辺11メートルの方墳である。墳丘中央に[[割竹形木棺]]を収めた粘土槨が遺存していた。棺内からは竪櫛3点、棺外から鉄刀1点が出土した。周溝内からは家形埴輪4点と土師器甕と高坏が出土し、土師器の型式から長原古墳群1期に位置づけられる。213号墳のすぐ北側には庄内式から布留式かけての円形周溝墓や[[方形周溝墓]]が分布しており、213号墳はこれらの墓域を踏襲して築かれた可能性が高い。長原古墳群ではこのように在来の集団によって営まれたものと117号墳のように、朝鮮半島南部の特徴をもつ土器（2期に属する）が出土する、渡来系の集団によって営まれた古墳が存在するようである。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 大阪府長原高廻り古墳群出土埴輪 一括（考古資料） - 明細は以下。所有者は国（文化庁）。大阪歴史博物館保管。1992年（平成4年）6月22日指定平成4年6月22日文部省告示第84号。文化庁サイトの「国指定文化財等データベース」「文化遺産オンライン」に指定年が「1929年（昭和4年）」とあるのは、「1992年（平成4年）」の誤り。。 ** 1号墳出土 *** 家 2箇 *** 船 1箇 *** 盾 1箇 *** 短甲 1箇 *** 草摺 1箇 *** 朝顔 2箇 *** 円筒 3箇 *** （附指定）鏃形土製品 3箇 ** 2号墳出土 *** 家 2箇 *** 船 1箇 *** 盾 2箇 *** 冑 1箇 *** 短甲 2箇 *** 草摺 2箇 *** 壺 6箇 *** 円筒 3箇 {{hidden begin |title = 国の重要文化財「大阪府長原高廻り古墳群出土埴輪」の画像 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} いずれも[[大阪歴史博物館]]展示。 ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 船形埴輪 (切妻)-1.JPG|家形埴輪（切妻） ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 船形埴輪 (切妻)-2.JPG|家形埴輪（切妻） ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 短甲形埴輪.JPG|短甲形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 草摺形埴輪.JPG|草摺形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 鏃形土製品.JPG|鏃形土製品 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|家形埴輪（入母屋） ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 船形埴輪 (切妻).JPG|家形埴輪（切妻） ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 盾形埴輪-1.JPG|盾形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 盾形埴輪-2.JPG|盾形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 冑形埴輪.JPG|冑形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 短甲形埴輪.JPG|短甲形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 草摺形埴輪-1.JPG|草摺形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 草摺形埴輪-2.JPG|草摺形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 壺形埴輪.JPG|壺形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪 {{hidden end}} === 大阪市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 長原40号墳出土盾形埴輪（考古資料） - 2001年（平成13年）12月21日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の大阪市ファイルより。。 ** 長原七ノ坪古墳出土副葬品（考古資料） - 2010年（平成22年）4月2日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/somu/cmsfiles/contents/0000074/74453/5471.pdf 平成22年4月2日大阪市公報]}}より大阪市教育委員会告示第13号（リンクは大阪市ホームページ）。。 {{hidden begin |title = 大阪市指定有形文化財「長原40号墳出土盾形埴輪」・「長原七ノ坪古墳出土副葬品」の画像 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} いずれも[[大阪歴史博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。 ファイル:長原40号墳出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|長原40号墳出土盾形埴輪 ファイル:長原七ノ坪古墳 出土品-1.JPG|長原七ノ坪古墳出土品 ファイル:長原七ノ坪古墳 出土品-2.JPG|長原七ノ坪古墳出土品 ファイル:長原七ノ坪古墳 出土品-3.JPG|長原七ノ坪古墳出土須恵器 {{hidden end}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == *[[長山雅一]] 「長原・加美古墳群」『新修大阪市史第一巻』 1988年 400-414頁 *長山雅一 「長原古墳群と畿内政権」『新修大阪市史第一巻』 1988年　448-454頁 *[[高島徹]] 「長原古墳群」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 424-425頁 *[[京嶋覚]] 「大複合遺跡、長原遺跡をめぐる」『なにわ考古学散歩』 大阪市文化財協会編 株式会社学生社 2007年 24-39頁　　 *[[寺井誠]] 「第ⅱ章　南・西南地区の調査」『長原遺跡発掘調査報告XV』 財団法人大阪市文化財協会 2006年　19-24頁 *[[寺井誠]] 「第ⅲ章　西地区の調査」『長原遺跡発掘調査報告XV』 財団法人大阪市文化財協会 2006年65頁 *寺井誠　「第Ⅳ章　中央地区の調査」『長原遺跡発掘調査報告XV』 財団法人大阪市文化財協会 2006年137頁 -142 *[[寺井誠]] 「長原古墳群集成」『長原遺跡発掘調査報告XV』 財団法人大阪市文化財協会 2006年 273-286頁 *寺井誠「長原の二大古墳」『大阪遺跡　出土品・遺構が語るなにわ発掘物語』　大阪市文化財協会編　創元社　2008年88頁-89頁 *加藤俊吾「七ノ坪古墳と南口古墳」『大阪遺跡　出土品・遺構が語るなにわ発掘物語』　大阪市文化財協会編　創元社　2008年　94頁-95頁 *京嶋覚「古墳時代の馬」『大阪遺跡　出土品・遺構が語るなにわ発掘物語』　大阪市文化財協会編　創元社　2008年　102頁-103頁 *大庭重信「長原古墳群」『シンポジウム大阪の初期群集墳を考える』　大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館　2006年　1頁-3頁 *三木弘『古墳社会と地域経営』学生社　2011年　239頁 == 関連項目 == *[[美園古墳]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05　長原高廻り1・2号墳の埴輪] （大阪文化財研究所） *[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html 長原七ノ坪古墳出土副葬品] （大阪市役所） *[http://obito1.web.fc2.com/nagahara03.html 大阪府大阪市・長原遺跡] （大和國古墳取調室） {{デフォルトソート:なかはらこふんくん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:平野区の歴史]] [[category:大阪市の重要文化財]] [[Category:群集墳]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[長原古墳群]] to [[Nagahara Kofun Cluster]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Infobox historic site | name = 長原古墳群 | native_name = | native_language = | image =Nagahara Kofun Group, sekihi.jpg | image_map = {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250}} | caption =長原古墳群 石碑（[[出戸駅]]前） | type =古墳群 | coordinates ={{coord|34|36|30.8|N|135|33|55.6|E|region:JP-27|display=inline,title}} | location =[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[平野区]]長吉長原 | area = | built = | architect = | architecture = | governing_body = | owner = | designation1 = | designation1_offname = | designation1_date = | designation1_number = }} {{Location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture| label = 長原古墳群| position = bottom| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{Center|位置図}}}} '''長原古墳群'''（ながはらこふんぐん）は、[[大阪府]][[大阪市]][[平野区]]長吉長原とその周辺の地下に埋没して存在する[[古墳群]]。[[古墳時代]]前期末から後期初めにかけて築かれた[[群集墳]]である。[[大阪市営地下鉄]]工事により存在が明らかになり、200基以上の古墳、古墳の痕跡が検出され、多数の[[埴輪]]が出土していることで有名となっている。 == 長原古墳群の発見と調査の経緯== 1973年（昭和48年）、[[大阪市営地下鉄]][[大阪市営地下鉄谷町線|谷町線]]延長計画に伴う長原遺跡の発掘調査で、地上には何ら古墳の痕跡がないのに[[円筒埴輪]]などが次々と出土した。調査を続けると墳丘の盛土の多くは失われているものの、墳裾に周溝を持つ小規模な古墳の痕跡が多数残っていることが判明した長山（1988）pp.400 - 401。 次いで、直径50メートル以上、残存部で2段に築かれた墳丘が姿を現し、その地区の小字名が塚ノ本であることから、塚ノ本古墳（円墳）と名付けられた。地籍図を調べると他にも、一ヶ塚、落塚、狐塚、高塚など周囲にかつて古墳が存在したことをうかがわせる地名が存在し、その範囲は南北2キロメートル、東西1.5キロメートルにも及んでいた長山（1988）pp.401 - 402。 これらの範囲では市営地下鉄工事の後も[[近畿自動車道]]の建設、区画整理事業、下水道工事、住宅建設、建築の建て替え工事などに伴い、続々と古墳の[[遺構]]が調査され、2006年までに調査、検出された数は213基にも及んでいる寺井（2006）p.273。 == 古墳群の形成時期== これらの古墳から出土した埴輪や[[須恵器]]の[[編年]]観から、4世紀末頃から6世紀前半にかけての期間にこの古墳群が形成されたと推定されており、これを基に4期にわたる変遷が説かれている長山（1988）p.405高島（1989）p.424。1期（4世紀末頃）には塚ノ本古墳や一ヶ塚古墳（[[帆立貝形古墳]]）など、直径数十メートルの比較的大型の古墳が築かれ、その周囲に2期から3期にかけて小規模な古墳（方墳）が多数築かれている。4期（6世紀前半頃）には[[前方後円墳]]の七ノ坪古墳や、南口古墳が築かれたのを最後にこの古墳群の造営は終了したのではないかとされる長山（1988）pp.404 - 415寺井（2006）pp.274 - 275。5世紀代とみられる2期と3期のものが多数を占めており、[[奈良県]][[新沢千塚古墳群]]などと同じく初期[[群集墳]]の1つに位置づけることが出来る長山（1988）pp.448 - 449。また長原古墳群が、南東わずか数キロに位置する日本最大級の古墳群、[[古市古墳群]]の造営時期とほぼ重なることから、両古墳群の関連も指摘されているところである。この事象と関連して、1期に比較的大型の盟主的古墳が築かれているのに2期から3期にはもっぱら小型の方墳ばかり築かれるのは、この地域の支配構造が1期は在地首長を通じて[[大王]]の間接的な支配を受けるものであったのが、2-3期には大王権による直接支配へと変化したことによるのではないかとする説もある大庭（2006）p.1。また、さらに進んで、長原古墳群の小形方墳の被葬者を[[ヤマト王権]]に組み込まれた原初的な「官僚」、初現的官僚組織に属した人々とする考えもある三木（2011）p.239。　 == 内部構造と遺物== [[ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 船形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|169号墳（高廻り1号墳）出土
船形埴輪（国の[[重要文化財]]）}}{{small|[[大阪歴史博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 船形埴輪.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|170号墳（高廻り2号墳）出土
船形埴輪（国の重要文化財）}}{{small|大阪歴史博物館展示。}}]] 長原古墳群では後世の削平が著しいため主体部（遺体が収められている内部構造）が残っているケースは少なく、その構造が明らかになっているのは18 - 19基である。それらは[[木棺直葬]]　[[円筒棺|埴輪棺]]（大型の円筒埴輪を棺に転用したものやあるいは遺体を収めるために作られた大型の埴輪）が主で、[[粘土槨]]も1例知られる。[[竪穴式石室]]や[[石棺]]の存在は現在のところ知られていないが、最後の時期に属する七ノ坪古墳（130号墳）からは[[横穴式石室]]の基底部が検出されている。 副葬品検出例は同じ理由で少ないが、鉄剣、鉄斧、鉄鎌などの鉄製品、[[須恵器]]、[[土師器]]、[[管玉]]などが出ており、上記、七ノ坪古墳の石室底部から馬具類一式がガラス玉、鉄製武器などともに出土している寺井（2006）p.275。 また、これらの古墳の墳丘の残存部や周溝から埴輪が多数出土しており、円筒埴輪、壺形埴輪の他に、人物、馬、[[盾]]、[[甲冑]]、蓋（きぬがさ）などの[[形象埴輪]]、囲形、舟形、家形の埴輪が出土している。 なかでも高廻り1号墳、2号墳（長原古墳群の支群の古墳群）から出土した船形、短甲形、冑形などの埴輪30点は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている京嶋（2007）p.29「新指定の文化財」『月刊文化財』号、第一法規、1992、pp.17 - 20, 42。 1号墳の船形埴輪は[[宮崎県]] [[西都原古墳群]]などで出土しているものと同じく、船首、船尾が反り上がったゴンドラ形をしていた。2号墳からは別のタイプの船の埴輪が出土しており、船首、船尾に波除板を付けるもので、隣接する[[八尾市]][[久宝寺遺跡]]より出土した木造船（準構造船）と同じ構造であった京嶋（2007）pp.29 - 30。 両古墳が検出された場所は現在、児童公園とされ、なみはや公園と呼ばれている。これは大阪市が市制100周年事業の1つとして2号墳の船形埴輪をモデルに復原船が製作され、公募によってこの船が「なみはや」と命名され、1989年（平成元年）に韓国釜山まで実験航海を行なったことに由来する（大阪[[天保山]]から出航し、約700キロの航海をもっぱら手漕ぎで、35日かかったという）。両古墳出土の埴輪の実物は[[大阪歴史博物館]]に展示されており、復原船「なみはや」の方は、[[なにわの海の時空館]]に展示されている。また、同公園には長原高廻り古墳群の顕彰碑が同市によって建てられている京嶋（2007）p.30。古墳時代、海と繋がる[[河内湖]]は奥深く内陸に入り込んでおり、長原の地も[[瀬戸内海]]や外洋と直接、繋がっていたようである。 File:Namihaya in Osaka Maritime Museum-2.jpg|古代船「なみはや」
{{small|[[なにわの海の時空館]]展示。}} ==調査で明らかにされた主要な古墳== ;塚ノ本古墳　　 :部分的な発掘調査であるが、直径55メートルの円墳で、周溝の幅は15メートルの規模であることが判明している。埋葬施設や葺石は確認されていないが、墳丘には埴輪が並んでおり、円筒・朝顔形・家形埴輪が出土している。家形埴輪は立派な円柱を持ち、2階建てで高さ80センチになる。長原古墳群では最も古い4世紀末の築造と推定される。古墳周溝のすぐ外では[[円筒棺]]や[[土壙墓]]が確認されている。これらの墓の中には勾玉、鉄釧などの装身具を有するものもあった。被葬者は塚ノ本古墳の被葬者に従属する立場にあった人々と思われる。 ;一ケ塚古墳 :部分的な調査しか行われていないが、方形の[[造出し]]を有する直径47メートルの円墳（または帆立貝形古墳）であり、造出しを含めると全長53メートルの規模である。周溝の幅は14メートルある。埋葬施設は確認されていないが、多種多様な埴輪が周溝から出土している。家形埴輪は7点以上出土しており、その1つは高さ80センチに復元される2階建ての入母屋造りである。この他、盾、[[靭]]、[[鞆]]、[[短甲]]、草摺、蓋などの形象埴輪も多く出土している。被葬者を来世に送る葬送儀礼を墳丘上に再現したものとされる。5世紀初頭頃の古墳と推定されている寺井（2007）pp.88-89。 ;七ノ坪古墳　 :全長約33メートルの帆立貝形の前方後円墳と推定されている。当古墳群のほとんどは墳丘を削られ主体部が失われている場合が多いが、横穴式石室の床部分が辛うじて残り、そこから馬具、鉄製武器、玉類、須恵器が出土した。石室は近畿地方に、横穴式石室の構造が伝えられた初期の形式に属する片袖式の構造である。 ;南口古墳　 :七ノ坪古墳と同じく帆立貝形の前方後円墳で全長は34．5メートルを測る。周溝から埴輪や須恵器、韓式系土器、土師器、刀形木製品、それに馬の骨が出土している。埴輪は円筒埴輪、人物埴輪、武人埴輪、家形埴輪、馬形埴輪、器財埴輪（甲冑、盾　蓋）が見られる。馬骨は両脚の四肢の部分がそのまま据えられた状態で検出され、墳丘の前方部裾部分からは歯の部分も見つかっている。これらのことから周溝内で馬の首を切り、その首を墳丘上においたことが想像され、馬供儀と呼ばれる葬送儀礼が行われたことを示唆している。このような例は長野県など古代の[[牧 (古代)|牧]]（馬の飼育場）があったとされる場所に近い地域の中小規模古墳に多くみられるものである加藤（2007）pp.94-95。 :[[平安時代]]、[[河内国]]にあった皇室の[[御牧]]の1つ、会賀牧（えがまき）が長原付近にあったという説がある京嶋（2008）pp.102-103。長原古墳群の周辺に広がる長原遺跡（旧石器時代から中世にまたがる[[複合遺跡]]で長原古墳群もこの遺跡に含まれる）からは[[治暦]]2年（1066年）の年号のある、土地の帰属をめぐる係争に関する[[木簡]]の文書が出土しており、このなかには「長原里」の地名などとともに末尾に「會賀御□」の記載がある。このことから現在の長原付近が、平安時代頃には、すでに長原里と呼ばれていることが分かり、さらに長原里が広大な[[皇室領]]であった会賀牧の範囲に含まれていたことが推定される寺井（2007）pp.137-142。長原から南に2キロに恵我之荘（えがのしょう-[[羽曳野市]]恵我之荘）の地名が現在も見られる。長原古墳群付近では古墳時代の村の跡からも馬の存在を示す歯や骨が出土し、また古墳群からも馬形埴輪が多く出土しているので長原付近には古墳時代にも馬の飼育や輸送・交通をつかさどる馬飼いと呼ばれる人々が住んでいたと思われる。 [[File:Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, sekihi.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|長原高廻り古墳群
（なみはや公園）}}]] ;長原高廻り1・2号墳 :（上記内部構造と遺物の欄および外部リンク参照） ;87号墳 :墳丘の北辺の長さ12.0メートルの方墳で、墳丘の高さは1.0メートルまで残存する。幅3.0メートル、長さ2.0メートル造り出しがあり、造り出しには円筒埴輪列が残っていた。西辺には須恵器大甕を転用した主体部が検出されているが棺内（大甕）からは副葬品や人骨は検出されていない。埴輪には円筒埴輪以外に、蓋、馬、人物、鶏、草摺などの形象埴輪が出土した。人物は巫女で丸顔をし、衣を右肩から垂らし、左腕を前方にのばし、何かを捧げもっているようである。馬形埴輪は顔と脚部分が欠損するが、鞍や障泥、輪鐙、面繋、手綱の表現がある飾り馬である。鶏形埴輪は雄鶏を実物大で作っており、鶏冠や目は粘土を貼り付け、羽や足は線刻で表現されている寺井（2006）pp.19-24。 ;117号墳 :1辺が10-11メートルの方墳で、周溝幅が最大で5メートルある。[[陶邑窯]]出土の須恵器の編年TK208型式に該当する須恵器坏身、坏蓋、耳両付短頸壺、壺が出土した。耳両付短頸壺は朝鮮半島南部（[[全羅道]]）に系譜が求められる形態である。長原2期に位置づけられる寺井（2006）p.65。 ;213号墳 :大阪市文化財協会が2002年度に発掘した1辺11メートルの方墳である。墳丘中央に[[割竹形木棺]]を収めた粘土槨が遺存していた。棺内からは竪櫛3点、棺外から鉄刀1点が出土した。周溝内からは家形埴輪4点と土師器甕と高坏が出土し、土師器の型式から長原古墳群1期に位置づけられる。213号墳のすぐ北側には庄内式から布留式かけての円形周溝墓や[[方形周溝墓]]が分布しており、213号墳はこれらの墓域を踏襲して築かれた可能性が高い。長原古墳群ではこのように在来の集団によって営まれたものと117号墳のように、朝鮮半島南部の特徴をもつ土器（2期に属する）が出土する、渡来系の集団によって営まれた古墳が存在するようである。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 大阪府長原高廻り古墳群出土埴輪 一括（考古資料） - 明細は以下。所有者は国（文化庁）。大阪歴史博物館保管。1992年（平成4年）6月22日指定平成4年6月22日文部省告示第84号。文化庁サイトの「国指定文化財等データベース」「文化遺産オンライン」に指定年が「1929年（昭和4年）」とあるのは、「1992年（平成4年）」の誤り。。 ** 1号墳出土 *** 家 2箇 *** 船 1箇 *** 盾 1箇 *** 短甲 1箇 *** 草摺 1箇 *** 朝顔 2箇 *** 円筒 3箇 *** （附指定）鏃形土製品 3箇 ** 2号墳出土 *** 家 2箇 *** 船 1箇 *** 盾 2箇 *** 冑 1箇 *** 短甲 2箇 *** 草摺 2箇 *** 壺 6箇 *** 円筒 3箇 {{hidden begin |title = 国の重要文化財「大阪府長原高廻り古墳群出土埴輪」の画像 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} いずれも[[大阪歴史博物館]]展示。 ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 船形埴輪 (切妻)-1.JPG|家形埴輪（切妻） ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 船形埴輪 (切妻)-2.JPG|家形埴輪（切妻） ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 短甲形埴輪.JPG|短甲形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 草摺形埴輪.JPG|草摺形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り1号墳出土 鏃形土製品.JPG|鏃形土製品 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 家形埴輪.JPG|家形埴輪（入母屋） ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 船形埴輪 (切妻).JPG|家形埴輪（切妻） ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 盾形埴輪-1.JPG|盾形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 盾形埴輪-2.JPG|盾形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 冑形埴輪.JPG|冑形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 短甲形埴輪.JPG|短甲形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 草摺形埴輪-1.JPG|草摺形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 草摺形埴輪-2.JPG|草摺形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 壺形埴輪.JPG|壺形埴輪 ファイル:長原高廻り2号墳出土 円筒埴輪.JPG|円筒埴輪 {{hidden end}} === 大阪市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 長原40号墳出土盾形埴輪（考古資料） - 2001年（平成13年）12月21日指定[http://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ、平成30年11月21日更新版）の大阪市ファイルより。。 ** 長原七ノ坪古墳出土副葬品（考古資料） - 2010年（平成22年）4月2日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/somu/cmsfiles/contents/0000074/74453/5471.pdf 平成22年4月2日大阪市公報]}}より大阪市教育委員会告示第13号（リンクは大阪市ホームページ）。。 {{hidden begin |title = 大阪市指定有形文化財「長原40号墳出土盾形埴輪」・「長原七ノ坪古墳出土副葬品」の画像 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} いずれも[[大阪歴史博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。 ファイル:長原40号墳出土 盾形埴輪.JPG|長原40号墳出土盾形埴輪 ファイル:長原七ノ坪古墳 出土品-1.JPG|長原七ノ坪古墳出土品 ファイル:長原七ノ坪古墳 出土品-2.JPG|長原七ノ坪古墳出土品 ファイル:長原七ノ坪古墳 出土品-3.JPG|長原七ノ坪古墳出土須恵器 {{hidden end}} == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == *[[長山雅一]] 「長原・加美古墳群」『新修大阪市史第一巻』 1988年 400-414頁 *長山雅一 「長原古墳群と畿内政権」『新修大阪市史第一巻』 1988年　448-454頁 *[[高島徹]] 「長原古墳群」『日本古墳大辞典』 株式会社東京堂出版 1989年 424-425頁 *[[京嶋覚]] 「大複合遺跡、長原遺跡をめぐる」『なにわ考古学散歩』 大阪市文化財協会編 株式会社学生社 2007年 24-39頁　　 *[[寺井誠]] 「第ⅱ章　南・西南地区の調査」『長原遺跡発掘調査報告XV』 財団法人大阪市文化財協会 2006年　19-24頁 *[[寺井誠]] 「第ⅲ章　西地区の調査」『長原遺跡発掘調査報告XV』 財団法人大阪市文化財協会 2006年65頁 *寺井誠　「第Ⅳ章　中央地区の調査」『長原遺跡発掘調査報告XV』 財団法人大阪市文化財協会 2006年137頁 -142 *[[寺井誠]] 「長原古墳群集成」『長原遺跡発掘調査報告XV』 財団法人大阪市文化財協会 2006年 273-286頁 *寺井誠「長原の二大古墳」『大阪遺跡　出土品・遺構が語るなにわ発掘物語』　大阪市文化財協会編　創元社　2008年88頁-89頁 *加藤俊吾「七ノ坪古墳と南口古墳」『大阪遺跡　出土品・遺構が語るなにわ発掘物語』　大阪市文化財協会編　創元社　2008年　94頁-95頁 *京嶋覚「古墳時代の馬」『大阪遺跡　出土品・遺構が語るなにわ発掘物語』　大阪市文化財協会編　創元社　2008年　102頁-103頁 *大庭重信「長原古墳群」『シンポジウム大阪の初期群集墳を考える』　大阪府立近つ飛鳥博物館　2006年　1頁-3頁 *三木弘『古墳社会と地域経営』学生社　2011年　239頁 == 関連項目 == *[[美園古墳]] == 外部リンク == *[http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05　長原高廻り1・2号墳の埴輪] （大阪文化財研究所） *[https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html 長原七ノ坪古墳出土副葬品] （大阪市役所） *[http://obito1.web.fc2.com/nagahara03.html 大阪府大阪市・長原遺跡] （大和國古墳取調室） {{デフォルトソート:なかはらこふんくん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[Category:平野区の歴史]] [[category:大阪市の重要文化財]] [[Category:群集墳]]

revision.text:
'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線|qq=draft}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|qq=draft}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権|qq=draft}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬|qq=draft}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

revision.text:
'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in {{ill|Hirano-ku|ja|平野区|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka City|ja|大阪市|qq=draft}}, [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線|qq=draft}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}}.
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
Among the discoveries was a large circular mound over 50 meters in diameter, with remnants indicating a two-tiered structure. This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|qq=draft}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権|qq=draft}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬|qq=draft}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

This tumulus, located in an area historically called "Tsukano-moto," was named the Tsukano-moto Kofun. Further examination of cadastral maps uncovered place names such as Ikka-zuka, Ochizuka, Kitsunezuka, and Takazuka, indicating the possible past presence of tumuli. The total area of the cluster spans approximately 2 kilometers north-south and 1.5 kilometers east-west<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.401-402</ref>.
As a result of construction projects, such as the {{ill|Kinki Expressway|ja|近畿自動車道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinki Expressway|qq=}}, urban planning initiatives, and residential developments, archaeological surveys continued, identifying 213 tumuli by 2006<ref>Terai (2006) p.273</ref>.
== Formation Period == The Nagahara Kofun Cluster is believed to have formed over four periods, from the late 4th century to the early 6th century, based on the chronology of haniwa and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} unearthed at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) p.405</ref><ref name="takashima424">Takashima (1989) p.424</ref>. 1. '''Phase 1 (late 4th century)''': Larger tumuli, such as the Tsukano-moto Kofun and Ikka-zuka Kofun (scallop-shaped keyhole tomb), were constructed. 2. '''Phases 2-3 (5th century)''': Smaller tumuli, primarily square-shaped, were built surrounding the earlier large tumuli. 3. '''Phase 4 (early 6th century)''': Tumuli such as the Shichinotsubo Kofun and Minamiguchi Kofun were constructed, marking the end of activity at the site<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.404-415</ref><ref name="#1">Terai (2006) 11.274-275</ref>.
The predominance of small square tumuli from the 5th century aligns the Nagahara Kofun Cluster with early cluster tumuli like the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}} in Nara. Furthermore, its contemporaneity with the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|ja|古市古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, one of Japan's largest kofun clusters, suggests a possible connection. The evolution from larger Phase 1 tumuli to smaller Phase 2-3 tumuli indicates a shift from indirect regional chieftain rule to direct authority by the {{ill|Yamato kingship|ja|大王権|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato kingship|qq=}}<ref name="#2">Ōba (2006) p.1</ref>. Some scholars propose that the buried individuals were primitive bureaucrats integrated into Yamato's administrative system<ref>Miki (2011) p.239</ref>.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Due to extensive later flattening, intact burial facilities are rare, with only 18–19 examples surviving. These primarily consist of {{ill|Wooden Coffin|lt=Wooden Coffin|lt=Wooden coffin burials|ja|木棺直葬|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffin burials|qq=}} or large cylindrical haniwa repurposed as coffins, with a single example of a clay sarcophagus. The Shichinotsubo Kofun (Tomb 130) features the foundation of an early corridor-style stone chamber, rare for the cluster<ref name="#1"/>.
Artifacts discovered include iron swords, axes, sickles, Sue pottery, {{ill|haji ware|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haji ware|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, beads, and horse fittings. Numerous haniwa have also been unearthed, including cylindrical, jar-shaped, and figurative types (e.g., human, horse, armor, shield, roof, and boat-shaped haniwa). Notably, boat-shaped haniwa from Tombs 1 and 2 of the Takamawari subgroup have been designated Important Cultural Properties by the Japanese government<ref>Kyojima (2007) p.29</ref><ref>Monthly Cultural Properties, 1992, 11.17-20, 42</ref>.
== Designated Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (National) === * {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniwa|qq=}} from the Nagahara Takamawari Kofun Group, including: ''' Tomb 1: house-shaped, boat-shaped, cylindrical haniwa, and more. ''' Tomb 2: house-shaped, boat-shaped, shield-shaped, and armor-shaped haniwa.
=== Osaka City Designated Cultural Properties === * Shield-shaped haniwa from Tomb 40. * Burial goods from Shichinotsubo Kofun.
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * Nagayama Masakazu, "Nagahara and Kami Kofun Clusters," in *New History of Osaka City, Vol. 1*, 1988. * Takashima Tetsu, "Nagahara Kofun Cluster," in *Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun*, Tokyo Publishing, 1989. * Terai Makoto, *Nagahara Ruins Excavation Report XV*, Osaka Cultural Properties Association, 2006. == See Also == * {{ill|Misono Kofun|ja|美園古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misono Kofun|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.occpa.or.jp/ikou/haniwa_gallery/ikou_01_01.html#gallery_05 Nagahara Takamawari Haniwa Gallery (Osaka Cultural Properties Research Institute)] * [https://www.city.osaka.lg.jp/kyoiku/page/0000074306.html Shichinotsubo Kofun Burial Goods (Osaka City)]

'''Nagahara Kofun Cluster''' (長原古墳群) refers to a group of [[late Kofun period]] tumuli located beneath Nagahara and its surrounding areas in [[:en:Hirano-ku, Osaka|Hirano-ku]], [[:en:Osaka|Osaka City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]]. This cluster, which dates from the late 4th to early 6th centuries, was discovered during the extension of the {{ill|Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|ja|大阪市営地下鉄谷町線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line|qq=}} and is known for the excavation of over 200 tumuli and numerous [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]].
== Discovery and Investigation of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster == The discovery of the Nagahara Kofun Cluster occurred in 1973, during an excavation survey prompted by the extension of the Osaka Municipal Subway Tanimachi Line. Although there were no visible surface traces of tumuli, cylindrical haniwa began to emerge, leading to further investigation. Subsequent surveys revealed numerous small tumuli remnants with circumferential moats, despite much of their mound soil having been lost<ref>Nagayama (1988) 11.400-401</ref>.
